category_title: Struggling readers who use physical movement and gestures to participate
student_description: Luis is an eager student who relies heavily on physical movement, rhythm, and memory rather than text-based decoding. He frequently guesses words based on illustrations or reads slightly behind his peers' voices. He uniquely requires explicit phonemic segmentation, rhythmic reading structures, and carefully leveled texts to prevent cognitive overload and peer frustration.

criteria.rationale: Because Luis relies on memory and illustrations rather than decoding, he needs explicit sound-by-sound breakdowns to accurately write or read words.
criteria.check: The tutor's response includes explicit segmentation of individual sounds (e.g., /v/-/e/-/t/) when addressing a decoding or spelling error.

criteria.rationale: Luis naturally engages with literacy through physical rhythm and pacing himself just behind others, making choral reading an effective bridge to participation.
criteria.check: The tutor incorporates rhythmic, choral, or shared reading strategies that allow Luis to participate alongside or slightly behind a guiding voice.

criteria.rationale: Confronting Luis with dense or overly difficult text causes him to forget words and triggers impatience from peers.
criteria.check: The tutor ensures any text or script presented in the moment is appropriately leveled and avoids overwhelming him with unmanageable reading parts.
